Protocol for Implementing Distributed Cox Regression on CCASAnet

for Mexico (participating site)

Author

Kaixing Liu

Published

August 8, 2025

Modified

August 13, 2025

1 Preparation

1.1 Action 1

  1. Create/Choose a directory in your local machine at your discretion.
    • For example, I created a local directory with absolute path, "/Users/lkx220/Desktop/mexico".
  2. Download from the cloud (e.g., OneDrive) the following items,
    • R scripts, distributed-analysis-ccasanet.R and authentic-distributed-analysis.R, to the local directory specified in 1., say "/Users/lkx220/Desktop/mexico";
    • site-specific individual-level data: Mexico shall download mexico_all_trt.rds and mexico_all_vl.rds to the same local directory;
    • global set-up file: setup.rds to the same local directory;
    • site-specific instruction: Mexico should download protocol-CCASAnet-Mexico.html to anywhere in your local machine (this file is meant to guide you the procedures of implementation of this distributed analysis);

1.1.1 Check

Make sure that R scripts, set-up file, and the data are in the same local directory, shown below.

This local directory (chosen at your discretion) would be the working directory in R later.

1.2 Action 2

  1. Open RStudio or R and set working directory as the local directory you chosen in Preparation.

    You may as well run the command below in your R console.

    # setwd("your-working-directory")
setwd("/Users/lkx220/Desktop/mexico") # my example

  2. Source the code and import data and set-up file.

    Please execute code below in console.

    source("distributed-analysis-ccasanet.R")
source("authentic-distributed-analysis.R")
mexico.vl = readRDS("mexico_all_vl.rds")
mexico.trt = readRDS("mexico_all_trt.rds")
setup = readRDS("setup.rds")
# prepare data for different scenarios
# for Mexico
## you can just ignore warning messages
mexico.vl.cc = mexico.vl %>% subset(!is.na(sex_mode) & !is.na(cd4_v_initial_sqrt) & !is.na(rna_v_initial_log))
mexico.vl.mi = mice.local.merge(mice(mexico.vl %>% select(all_of(setup[["vl.imp.var"]])), m=setup[["no.of.imputation"]], method = "pmm",
                                 seed = setup[["seed"]], printFlag = FALSE), m=setup[["no.of.imputation"]])
mexico.trt.cc = mexico.trt %>% subset(!is.na(sex_mode) & !is.na(cd4_v_initial_sqrt) & !is.na(rna_v_initial_log))
mexico.trt.mi = mice.local.merge(mice(mexico.trt %>% select(all_of(setup[["trt.imp.var"]])), m=setup[["no.of.imputation"]], method = "pmm",
                                 seed = setup[["seed"]], printFlag = FALSE), m=setup[["no.of.imputation"]])

2 Implementation

2.1 Virologic Failure, Complete-Case Analysis

  • Initialization
  • Derivation

—Wait for Chile to upload control.json file to the cloud.—

  1. After downloading control.json along with the "vl/complete-case" directory in the cloud to local directory.

    CHECK

    and

    the “step” in control.json file in "./vl/complete-case" should be “initialize” (you can just open the control.json file or preview it if possible).

    Then run the code below.

    set.seed(setup[["seed"]])
pda.local(mexico.vl.cc, "mexico", getwd(), m = 1, complete = TRUE, outcome = "vl")

    CHECK

  2. Mexico uploads this mexico_initialize.json to "./vl/complete-case" in the cloud.

—Wait for Chile to update control.json file in the cloud.—

  1. Mexico downloads control.json file again from "./vl/complete-case" in the cloud to local "./vl/complete-case".

    CHECK

    The “step” in control.json file in "./vl/complete-case" should be “derive” now (you can just open the control.json file or preview it if possible).

  2. Mexico runs command below.

    set.seed(setup[["seed"]])
pda.derive(mexico.vl.cc, "mexico", getwd(), m = 1, complete = TRUE, outcome = "vl")

    CHECK

  3. Mexico uploads mexico_derive.json in local "./vl/complete-case" to "./vl/complete-case" in the cloud.

—You are Done for federated analysis in this scenario.—

Now you probably get familiar with the procedure.

For virologic failure with multiple imputation later, the procedures are the same overall except that we will do this per imputed data set.

No worries. The command given below realize this in parallel and this means you still just need run it once.

But you need do more when downloading and uploading files since we have multiple imputed data sets.

2.2 Virologic Failure, with Multiple Imputation

  • Initialization
  • Derivation

—Wait for Chile to upload control.json file in OneDrive.—

  1. After downloading control.json along with the number-indexed directory in OneDrive to local directory,

    CHECK

    and

    the “step” in control.json file in "./vl/1" should be “initialize” (you can just open the control.json file or preview it if possible).

    Then run the command below

    set.seed(setup[["seed"]])
pda.local(mexico.vl.mi, "mexico", getwd(), m = setup[["no.of.imputation"]], outcome="vl")

    CHECK

  2. Mexico uploads mexico_initialize.json in each local number-indexed directory to corresponding number-indexed directory in the cloud.

—Wait for Chile to update control.json file.—

  1. Mexico downloads control.json file in each number-indexed directory from the cloud to corresponding local number-indexed directory.

    CHECK

    and

    the “step” in control.json file in "./vl/1" should be “derive” (you can just open the control.json file or preview it if possible).

  2. Mexico runs command below.

    set.seed(setup[["seed"]])
pda.derive(mexico.vl.mi, "mexico", getwd(), m = setup[["no.of.imputation"]], outcome = "vl")

    CHECK (mexico_derive.json)

  3. Mexico uploads mexico_derive.json in each number-indexed directory to corresponding number-indexed directory in the cloud.

—You are Done for federated analysis in this scenario.—

Regarding Major Regimen Change, we just repeat the above procedures.

2.3 Major Regimen Change, Complete-Case Analysis

  • Initialization
  • Derivation

—Wait for Chile to upload control.json file to the cloud.—

  1. After downloading control.json along with the "trt/complete-case" directory in the cloud to local directory.

    CHECK similarly

    and

    the “step” in control.json file in "./trt/complete-case" should be “initialize” (you can just open the control.json file or preview it if possible).

    Then run the code below.

    set.seed(setup[["seed"]])
pda.local(mexico.trt.cc, "mexico", getwd(), m = 1, complete = TRUE, outcome = "trt")

    CHECK similarly

  2. Mexico uploads this mexico_initialize.json to "./trt/complete-case" in the cloud.

—Wait for Chile to update control.json file in the cloud.—

  1. Mexico downloads control.json file again from "./trt/complete-case" in the cloud to local "./trt/complete-case".

    CHECK

    The “step” in control.json file in "./trt/complete-case" should be “derive” now (you can just open the control.json file or preview it if possible).

  2. Mexico runs command below.

    set.seed(setup[["seed"]])
pda.derive(mexico.trt.cc, "mexico", getwd(), m = 1, complete = TRUE, outcome = "trt")

    CHECK similarly

  3. Mexico uploads mexico_derive.json in local "./trt/complete-case" to "./trt/complete-case" in the cloud.

—You are Done for federated analysis in this scenario.—

Now you probably get familiar with the procedure.

For major regimen change with multiple imputation later, the procedures are the same overall except that we will do this per imputed data set.

No worries. The command given below realize this in parallel and this means you still just need run it once.

But you need do more when downloading and uploading files since we have multiple imputed data sets.

2.4 Major Regimen Change, with Multiple Imputation

  • Initialization
  • Derivation

—Wait for Chile to upload control.json file in OneDrive.—

  1. After downloading control.json along with the number-indexed directory in OneDrive to local directory,

    CHECK similarly

    and

    the “step” in control.json file in "./trt/1" should be “initialize” (you can just open the control.json file or preview it if possible).

    Then run the command below

    set.seed(setup[["seed"]])
pda.local(mexico.trt.mi, "mexico", getwd(), m = setup[["no.of.imputation"]], outcome="trt")

    CHECK similarly

  2. Mexico uploads mexico_initialize.json in each local number-indexed directory to corresponding number-indexed directory in the cloud.

—Wait for Chile to update control.json file.—

  1. Mexico downloads control.json file in each number-indexed directory from the cloud to corresponding local number-indexed directory.

    CHECK similarly

    and

    the “step” in control.json file in "./trt/1" should be “derive” (you can just open the control.json file or preview it if possible).

  2. Mexico runs command below.

    set.seed(setup[["seed"]])
pda.derive(mexico.trt.mi, "mexico", getwd(), m = setup[["no.of.imputation"]], outcome = "trt")

    CHECK similarly (mexico_derive.json)

  3. Mexico uploads mexico_derive.json in each number-indexed directory to corresponding number-indexed directory in the cloud.

—You are Done for federated analysis in this scenario.—
